title: Microsoft Device Code Phishing Bypasses MFA Using Legitimate Login Flow
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-08, Reading time: 3 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.theregister.com%2F2026%2F04%2F07%2Fmicrosoft_device_code_phishing%2F%3Futm_source=tldrit/1/0100019d6d02c3e9-c23c891f-064f-46d8-9146-a29075176e5d-000000/BRHFKbdVELZzICObt-2lnqxx74KS29-btLhNo0NbgyI=452
summary: Microsoft Device Code Phishing Bypasses MFA Using Legitimate Login Flow (3 minute read) A large-scale campaign is abusing Microsoft's device code authentication flow to trick users into granting access, allowing attackers to capture tokens and maintain persistent access without needing passwords. This attack targets the authorization layer, meaning MFA and even passwordless methods don't stop it once a user completes the flow on a legitimate login page.

title: Most AI infra bets still aren't paying off
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-08, Reading time: 3 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.theregister.com%2F2026%2F04%2F07%2Fai_returns_gartner%3Futm_source=tldrit/1/0100019d6d02c3e9-c23c891f-064f-46d8-9146-a29075176e5d-000000/3jHLTEnwVbLOYTSQOlZ4K9OEcpHtNAGWOpd4b4CnFI8=452

summary: Agent Skills Are the New SDK (And You Should Be Building One) (16 minute read) Developer infrastructure companies need to adjust their product for AI coding agents as a competitive necessity. title: 2026 state of AI agents
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-08, Reading time: 15 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.databricks.com%2Fsites%2Fdefault%2Ffiles%2F2026-01%2FState-of-AI-Agents-2026-Final.pdf%3Futm_source=tldrfounders/1/0100019d6d1b660e-b97ca0e7-4685-4bb6-9935-649f9c2c2e9e-000000/-acPtwZ9bNPWu9KIjigF0FbRXEcM6nsHto0FiMkHG7g=452
summary: 2026 state of AI agents (15 minute read) The numbers in Databricks' annual State of AI Agents report are striking. Companies with AI governance frameworks pushed 12x more projects to production. Of the top 15 enterprise use cases, 40% center on customer experience and engagement.

summary: From Signup to Value: How AI Is Changing Activation in SaaS (12 minute read) AI has changed what's possible in the first session. Strong first outputs, self-building setup, and onboarding that adapts to what users actually say they need are real improvements.

title: Vibe → Environment → Culture: Leadership Gets It Backwards
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-08, Reading time: 3 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Flinks.tldrnewsletter.com%2FsVw2Ms/1/0100019d6d1b660e-b97ca0e7-4685-4bb6-9935-649f9c2c2e9e-000000/7ux4Tl8mSiJc5BroOZ0YOLiOauu33nlQ49fGgRUlDw4=452
summary: Vibe → Environment → Culture: Leadership Gets It Backwards (3 minute read) Leaders obsess over culture when they should start with their vibe. That vibe shapes the environment (processes, approvals, and work patterns), and culture naturally emerges from that environment. Misaligned environments frustrate A-players, slow output, and create politics. The solution is explicit leadership, environment design, and clarity, not trying to force culture directly.

title: GLM-5.1: Towards Long-Horizon Tasks
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-08, Reading time: 14 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fz.ai%2Fblog%2Fglm-5.1%3Futm_source=tldrai/1/0100019d6d40f309-969c6a52-ea10-424c-ac26-61ab46943a0f-000000/66eWMoclQIKP_qoNLz6SpTGfkm6q8CojC3fcy0qwUko=452
summary: GLM-5.1: Towards Long-Horizon Tasks (14 minute read) GLM-5.1 is a flagship model for agentic engineering created by Z.ai. It achieves state-of-the-art performance on SWE-Bench Pro. The model is built to stay effective on agent tasks over much longer horizons than previous generations. It can sustain optimization over hundreds of rounds and thousands of tool calls. title: Understanding Traceroute
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-10, Reading time: 16 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Ftech.stonecharioteer.com%2Fposts%2F2026%2Ftraceroute%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdevops/1/0100019d7727cfb7-98c15ebb-5c93-4217-b8cf-2b48fe4e8b5f-000000/9aX_6cuZFH0JffwltVDizsFuSVRdpCFI6zt24fBOfLQ=452
summary: Understanding Traceroute (16 minute read) Traceroute works by sending packets with increasing TTL values so each router drops them in sequence and returns an ICMP “Time Exceeded” message, revealing each hop's IP until the destination responds with a different ICMP message. By repeating this process with multiple probes and measuring response times, it maps the path and latency of network traffic, though gaps occur when routers don't send replies.

title: Venture Capital Has a Starting Line Problem

summary: Venture Capital Has a Starting Line Problem (7 minute read) Venture capital often favors well-networked founders who start with advantages like connections and financial runway, leaving others at a disadvantage from the start. This disparity isn't about in-room investor bias, but rather the different starting lines shaped by existing networks and resources. Underrepresented founders must proactively build credibility within these ecosystems to secure funding, despite systemic inequities.

title: Keeping a Postgres queue healthy
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-13, Reading time: 17 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fplanetscale.com%2Fblog%2Fkeeping-a-postgres-queue-healthy%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019d86505a37-4eab1be6-91fc-4f02-9726-1f326edbfe9a-000000/O9orq_oemFoBS0T4am4xvPjp0MP5uUi0DVjQYhD1GKo=452
summary: Keeping a Postgres queue healthy (17 minute read) Postgres job queues often degrade not because of performance limits, but because deleted rows (“dead tuples”) pile up when cleanup (vacuuming) is blocked by long or overlapping queries from other workloads. Over time, this creates hidden overhead and slows everything down. The solution is to control and limit competing query traffic so vacuuming can run effectively, keeping the queue and database stable.

title: Why Shannon Entropy Catches What Schema Validation Misses
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-13, Reading time: 8 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fetherealogic.ai%2Fwhy-shannon-entropy-catches-what-schema-validation-misses%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019d86505a37-4eab1be6-91fc-4f02-9726-1f326edbfe9a-000000/FLA8qQAOuASNTLNBkbZd5j8taOuEb5M0Iir9n6Ma8Uk=452
summary: Why Shannon Entropy Catches What Schema Validation Misses (8 minute read) Traditional data checks can pass even when pipelines are semantically broken: schema, row count, null rates, and freshness don't detect distribution collapse, over-merging, or silent information loss. Shannon entropy can be used as a signal-integrity metric to monitor drift over time or information preservation across transformations.

title: Your AI Dashboard Looks Cool. Nobody Learns Anything From It
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-13, Reading time: 9 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fmotherduck.com%2Fblog%2Fvibecoding-dashboards-best-practices%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019d86505a37-4eab1be6-91fc-4f02-9726-1f326edbfe9a-000000/wunENFiPGwaJMjclMO-4t8HVCQAUR-NUVNSYU5FsjFw=452
summary: Your AI Dashboard Looks Cool. Nobody Learns Anything From It (9 minute read) To build a useful dashboard with AI, start with a clear question to keep it focused (one main insight per view), match the chart type to the question type, and use descriptive names and comments for AI to understand your intent. Finally, make sure your AI dashboard tells a story instead of just displaying numbers.

title: The broken economics of databases
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-04-13, Reading time: 9 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.bitsxpages.com%2Fp%2Fthe-broken-economics-of-databases%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019d86505a37-4eab1be6-91fc-4f02-9726-1f326edbfe9a-000000/pYOIdL9HoMaSmB4KdzwxYJhyZDGylQUtOCs1rtRYJ-Q=452
summary: The broken economics of databases (9 minute read) Database vendors look insanely profitable on gross margin, yet stay barely profitable because R&D and go-to-market costs are huge. As databases commoditize and hyperscalers own the infrastructure, vendors defend margins with differentiation, pricing opacity, and growing operational complexity. Net effect for data engineers: products often get more features but not simpler operations, because complexity itself helps preserve vendor economics.
